summary: This video compilation contains multiple unrelated segments: VR headset demonstrations (HTC Vive), conference/educational event footage, anatomical 3D models, surgical simulator setup, real surgical footage (appears to be robotic/minimally invasive procedure), and institutional branding. No single FLS task performance is clearly identifiable.

content_segments_identified.description: Hands-on anatomical model palpation (appears to be neurosurgical anatomy)
3D anatomical renderings of brain with surgical instruments
Surgical simulator (Simbionix/da Vinci) setup with trainee at controls
Real surgical footage showing robotic/minimally invasive technique on tissue
Laparoscopic trainer display showing peg transfer task
content_segments_identified.note: Camera view of FLS trainer screen showing pegboard setup with pegs and flat surface, but no actual performance being scored

content_segments_identified.description: University of Nebraska Medical Center branding

reason: This is a compilation/montage video with educational, promotional, and institutional content. No singular FLS task performance by a trainee is presented in scorable form. While segment 7 shows an FLS pegboard trainer on a monitor, no actual trainee performance is recorded. This classification is 'instructional' (educational montage) rather than 'performance'.
recommendation: To score an FLS task, provide a video of a single trainee performing one complete task on an FLS trainer with continuous camera view of the task field and timing information.
